title: Parliamentary activity | Damian Hinds
meta_description: Damian was first elected as the Conservative Member of Parliament for East Hampshire in May 2010. He was re-elected to this position in May 2015, 2017 and again in December 2019.

text_excerpt: Parliamentary activity | Damian Hinds Skip to main content Damian Hinds MP for East Hampshire Main navigation Home About News Campaigns In Parliament Surgeries, advice and public meetings Contact Tickets for PMQs and tours of Parliament Damian Hinds MP for East Hampshire In Parliament Parliamentary activity Tweet On this page, you can see Damian's latest activity in Parliament, including speeches and written questions to Ministerial departments. Oral or Written Questions Once a month Ministers from each government department answer questions from MPs at the Dispatch Box. There is a limit to the number of questions that can be asked. MPs can also table a written question to the relevant Government department, which are published in Hansard. http://www.parliament.uk/business/publications/hansard/ Adjournment Debate MPs may be able to raise a constituent's issue in a half-hour Adjournment Debate. To get an adjournment debate, MPs must be successful in a ballot of Members of Parliament or have the subject chosen by the Speaker. The debates are usually the last business of the day and a government minister responds at the end of the debate. Petitions Members of Parliament can present a petition to Parliament on behalf of their constituents. The format and wording of the petition need to be in a particular way. For more information or guidance, please visit: Petitions - UK Government and Parliament Damian's activity in parliament Damian is currently a member of the Select Committee on Culture, Media and Sport, is co-chair of the All Party Parliamentary Group on Down Syndrome and is a member of a number of All Party Parliamentary Groups including heritage railways, rural services, children's online safety, hospitality and tourism, haemophilia and contaminated blood, AI and youth employment. He also recently sat on Bill Committees for the Children's Wellbeing and Schools Bill and the IfATE Bill (now Act). This month, Damian has asked the following written questions (click the links below to see the answers from the Government): Written Answer - Department for Health and Social Care (25 June 2025) Damian Hinds: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if she will make an assessment of the potential merits of awarding Dental Schools status to the University of Portsmouth Dental Academy. Written Answers - HM Treasury (23 June 2025) Damian Hinds: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, if she will make an assessment of the potential merits of merging the Valuation Office Agency and HMRC when dealing with businesses, in the context of changes to Agricultural Property Relief. Written Answers - Home Office (4 June 2025) Damian Hinds: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what recent discussions she has had with Police and Crime Commissioners on tackling vehicle nuisance in residential areas. Written Answers - Home Office (4 June 2025) Damian Hinds: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps she is taking to give powers to the police to tackle illegally modified exhausts for (a) cars and (b) motorcycles. Written Answers - Department for Transport (4 June 2025) Damian Hinds: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 8 May to Question HL6929 on Roads: Cameras, if she will issue guidance to (a) police and (b) local authorities on how roadside noise cameras may be used to tackle noise-related anti-social behaviour. Written Answers - Department for Education (3 June 2025) Damian Hinds: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, how much (a) set up, (b) other one-off, (c) food, (d) staffing and (e) other operating cost funding has been allocated to schools for school breakfast pilots in total; and what estimate she has made of the (i) number of eligible children and (ii) average number of breakfasts to be taken per eligible child over the period.
